member.name: T:Enru.Yantra.Commands.CreateContainerReport.PartitionExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for partitioning sequences into lists of specified sizes.
member.remarks: These methods enable flexible partitioning strategies for enumerable collections, such as dividing items evenly or creating partitions suitable for reporting scenarios. The returned partitions are materialized as lists, and the original sequence is enumerated only once. All methods are implemented as extension methods for any enumerable type.

member.name: M:Enru.Yantra.Commands.CreateContainerReport.PartitionExtensions.PartitionEvenly``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Partitions the elements of a sequence into lists of up to a specified maximum number of items per partition.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the source sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to partition. Cannot be null.
The maximum number of items allowed in each partition. Must be greater than zero.
member.returns: An enumerable collection of lists, where each list contains up to the specified maximum number of items from the source sequence. The last list may contain fewer items if the total number of elements is not evenly divisible.

member.name: M:Enru.Yantra.Commands.CreateContainerReport.PartitionExtensions.PartitionForReport``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Partitions the source sequence into a series of lists for reporting, using specified sizes for the first, middle, and last partitions.
member.remarks: If the total number of elements is less than or equal to firstPartitionSize, a single partition containing all elements is returned. The method enumerates the source sequence immediately and buffers it in memory.
member.typeparam: The type of elements in the source sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to partition. Cannot be null.
The number of elements in the first partition. Must be greater than 0.
The number of elements in each middle partition. Must be greater than 0.
The number of elements in the last partition. Must be greater than 0.
member.returns: An enumerable collection of lists, where each list contains a partition of the source sequence. The first and last partitions have the specified sizes, and any remaining elements are divided into partitions of the given middle size.
member.exception: Thrown if firstPartitionSize, partitionSize, or lastPartitionSize is less than or equal to 0.

member.name: M:Enru.Yantra.Commands.CreateContainerReport.PartitionExtensions.PartitionForReport``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Partitions the source sequence into a first partition of a specified size, followed by subsequent partitions of another specified size.
member.remarks: This method enumerates the source sequence eagerly to determine partition boundaries. The last partition may contain fewer elements than the specified partition size if there are not enough remaining elements.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the source sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to partition. Cannot be null.
The number of elements in the first partition. Must be greater than 0.
The number of elements in each subsequent partition. Must be greater than 0.
member.returns: An enumerable collection of lists, where the first list contains up to the specified number of elements for the first partition, and each subsequent list contains up to the specified partition size. If the source sequence is empty, the result is empty.
member.exception: Thrown if firstPartitionSize or partitionSize is less than or equal to 0.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.Pdf
member.summary: Provides static helper methods for constructing and configuring PDF section objects in a composable manner.
member.remarks: The Pdf class offers a fluent API for building complex PDF document structures by composing, styling, and configuring sections. It includes methods for creating common section types, setting styles, managing section hierarchies, and applying content or layout options. These extension methods are intended to simplify the creation and customization of PDF documents using strongly-typed models. All methods are static and are designed to be used with types implementing IPdfSection<TModel>;.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Pdf.WithStyleManager``1(PdfDocuments.IPdfSection{``0},PdfDocuments.IPdfStyleManager{``0})
member.summary: Assigns the specified style manager to the PDF section and returns the updated section.
member.remarks: This method enables fluent configuration of a PDF section's style manager. The section's existing style manager, if any, will be replaced.
member.typeparam: The type of the model associated with the PDF section. Must implement .
member.param: The PDF section to which the style manager will be assigned. Cannot be null.
The style manager to assign to the section. Cannot be null.
member.returns: The same PDF section instance with the specified style manager assigned.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Pdf.SetKey``1(PdfDocuments.IPdfSection{``0})
member.summary: Sets the section's key to a string derived from its type name, excluding common suffixes.
member.remarks: This method removes the 'Pdf', 'Section', and generic type suffixes from the section's type name to generate a concise key. The key is intended to uniquely identify the section based on its type.
member.typeparam: The model type associated with the PDF section. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.
member.param: The PDF section whose key will be set. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Pdf.VerticalStackSection``1
member.summary: Creates a section that arranges PDF content vertically in a stack.
member.remarks: Use this method to compose multiple PDF elements in a vertical layout. The section can be further configured or populated with child elements as needed.
member.typeparam: The type of the model used to bind data to the PDF section. Must implement IPdfModel.
member.returns: An IPdfSection<TModel> instance representing a vertically stacked section in the PDF document.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Pdf.VerticalStackSection``1(PdfDocuments.IPdfSection{``0}[])
member.summary: Creates a section that arranges its child sections vertically in the PDF document.
member.typeparam: The type of the model associated with the PDF section. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.
member.param: An array of child sections to be stacked vertically. Cannot be null; each element represents a section to include in the vertical stack.
member.returns: A PDF section that displays the specified child sections in a vertical arrangement.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Pdf.HorizontalStackSection``1
member.summary: Creates a section that arranges PDF elements horizontally in a stack.
member.remarks: Use this method to build layouts where elements are placed side by side within a PDF document. The section can be customized by adding child elements and configuring their properties.
member.typeparam: The type of the model associated with the PDF section. Must implement IPdfModel.
member.returns: An IPdfSection<TModel> instance that stacks its child elements horizontally.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Pdf.HorizontalStackSection``1(PdfDocuments.IPdfSection{``0}[])
member.summary: Creates a section that arranges child PDF sections horizontally in a stack.
member.remarks: The order of the child sections determines their placement from left to right. This method is useful for building composite layouts where multiple sections need to appear in a single row.
member.typeparam: The type of the model associated with each PDF section. Must implement IPdfModel.
member.param: An array of child sections to be arranged horizontally. Cannot be null.
member.returns: A section that displays the specified child sections side by side in a horizontal layout.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Pdf.OverlayStackSection``1
member.summary: Creates a new overlay stack section for PDF generation using the specified model type.
member.typeparam: The type of model used for the PDF section. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.
member.returns: An IPdfSection<TModel> instance representing an overlay stack section for the given model type.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Pdf.OverlayStackSection``1(PdfDocuments.IPdfSection{``0}[])
member.summary: Creates a section that overlays multiple PDF sections in a stack, allowing their content to be rendered together.
member.remarks: Use this method to combine multiple sections visually in a single layer. The resulting section renders all child sections together, which can be useful for composite layouts or layered content.
member.typeparam: The type of the model used by the PDF sections. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.
member.param: An array of PDF sections to be overlaid in the stack. The order of sections determines their stacking sequence.
member.returns: A PDF section representing the overlay of the specified child sections.

member.summary: Adds a data column to the PDF section with the specified header, binding expression, relative width, format, and style names.

member.summary: Adds a data column to the PDF section with customizable header, binding, width, format, and styles.

member.param: The PDF section whose child keys will be updated to include their hierarchical path. Cannot be null.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfBoundsExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for manipulating and aligning PDF bounds and coordinates.
member.remarks: The methods in this class support common operations for positioning, normalizing, and adjusting PDF layout bounds. These extensions are intended to simplify layout calculations when working with PDF grids, pages, and alignment scenarios.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfBoundsExtensions.AlignHorizontally(PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfHorizontalAlignment)
member.summary: Calculates the top-left point for horizontally aligning the inner bounds within the outer bounds according to the specified alignment.
member.remarks: The vertical position is always aligned to the top of the outer bounds. This method does not modify the original bounds; it only calculates the alignment point.
member.param: The bounds within which the inner bounds will be aligned. Represents the container area.
The bounds to be aligned horizontally inside the outer bounds. Represents the content area.
The horizontal alignment to apply when positioning the inner bounds within the outer bounds. Supported values are Left, Center, and Right.
member.returns: A PdfPoint representing the top-left position where the inner bounds should be placed to achieve the specified horizontal alignment within the outer bounds.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfBoundsExtensions.AlignVertically(PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfVerticalAlignment)
member.summary: Calculates the top-left point for positioning the inner bounds within the outer bounds according to the specified vertical alignment.
member.remarks: The returned point always uses the left column of the outer bounds for horizontal positioning. Use this method to determine the starting row for vertical alignment scenarios such as top, center, or bottom placement.
member.param: The bounds within which the inner bounds are to be aligned. Represents the container area.
The bounds to be aligned vertically inside the outer bounds. Represents the content area.
The vertical alignment to apply when positioning the inner bounds within the outer bounds. Specifies whether to align to the top, center, or bottom.
member.returns: A PdfPoint representing the top-left position where the inner bounds should be placed within the outer bounds based on the specified vertical alignment.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfBoundsExtensions.WithTopRow(PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the top row of the specified bounds and returns a normalized result.
member.remarks: Normalization ensures the bounds are valid after updating the top row. The original bounds instance is not modified.
member.param: The bounds to update with the new top row value.
The row index to set as the top row. Must be within the valid range for the bounds.
member.returns: A new instance of PdfBounds with the top row set to the specified value and normalized.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfBoundsExtensions.WithLeftColumn(PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a new set of PDF bounds with the left column set to the specified value.
member.remarks: Normalization ensures the resulting bounds are valid and consistent. Use this method to adjust the left boundary while preserving other bound properties.
member.param: The current PDF bounds to modify.
The column index to set as the left boundary. Must be a valid column index within the bounds.
member.returns: A normalized PdfBounds instance with the left column updated to the specified value.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfBoundsExtensions.Normalize(PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Returns a normalized copy of the specified bounds, ensuring all values are within valid ranges.
member.remarks: Normalization sets negative column or row indices to 0, and column or row counts less than or equal to 0 to 1. This helps prevent invalid bounds when working with PDF layouts.
member.param: The bounds to normalize. Column and row indices must be non-negative; column and row counts must be positive.
member.returns: A new PdfBounds instance with non-negative column and row indices, and positive column and row counts.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfBoundsExtensions.SubtractBounds``1(PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,``0,PdfDocuments.PdfSpacing)
member.summary: Calculates a new set of bounds by subtracting the specified inner spacing from the given outer bounds.
member.typeparam: The type of the PDF model used for grid calculations. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.
member.param: The original bounds from which the inner spacing will be subtracted.
The PDF grid page associated with the bounds calculation.
The PDF model instance used for grid calculations.
The spacing to subtract from the outer bounds. Specifies the amount to remove from each side.
member.returns: A PdfBounds instance representing the adjusted bounds after subtracting the specified inner spacing.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfBoundsExtensions.AddBounds``1(PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,``0,PdfDocuments.PdfSpacing)
member.summary: Creates a new set of bounds by expanding the specified outer bounds using the provided inner spacing values.
member.typeparam: The type of the model associated with the PDF grid page. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.
member.param: The original bounds to be expanded.
The PDF grid page to which the bounds and model are related.
The model instance associated with the PDF grid page.
The spacing values to apply to each side of the outer bounds when expanding.
member.returns: A new PdfBounds instance representing the expanded bounds after applying the specified inner spacing.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfDebugModeExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for working with the DebugMode enumeration, enabling convenient flag manipulation and checking.
member.remarks: These extension methods simplify common operations when using DebugMode as a bit field, such as determining whether a specific flag is set or updating flags. The methods are intended for use with DebugMode values that represent combinations of flags.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfDebugModeExtensions.HasFlag(PdfDocuments.DebugMode,PdfDocuments.DebugMode)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified debug mode value includes the given flag.
member.remarks: Use this method to test whether one or more flags are present in a composite DebugMode value. This is useful when DebugMode is defined as a bit field enumeration.
member.param: The debug mode value to evaluate for the presence of the specified flag.
The flag to check for within the debug mode value.
member.returns: true if all bits in checkFlag are set in debugMode; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfDebugModeExtensions.SetFlag(PdfDocuments.DebugMode,PdfDocuments.DebugMode,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets or clears the specified debug flag in the given debug mode value.
member.param: The current debug mode value to modify.
The debug flag to set or clear within the debug mode value.
A value indicating whether to set () or clear () the specified flag. Defaults to .
member.returns: A new value with the specified flag set or cleared as indicated.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageImageExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for drawing images onto a PDF grid page with fixed dimensions or alignment options.
member.remarks: These extension methods enable rendering images within a grid-based layout on PDF pages, supporting both fixed width and height scaling, as well as alignment within specified bounds. Methods accept either image file paths or preloaded image objects, allowing flexibility in image sourcing. All methods require a valid grid page context and appropriate grid coordinates or bounds for placement. Thread safety is not guaranteed; ensure proper synchronization if used in multi-threaded scenarios.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageImageExtensions.DrawImageWithFixedWidth(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Draws an image onto the PDF grid page, scaling it to fit a specified number of columns starting at the given grid position.
member.remarks: The image is scaled proportionally to fit the specified column width, preserving its aspect ratio. The top-left corner of the image aligns with the specified grid cell. If the image file is invalid or cannot be loaded, an exception may be thrown.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the image will be drawn.
The file path of the image to be rendered. Must reference a valid image file.
The index of the leftmost column where the image will be placed. Must be within the grid's column range.
The index of the top row where the image will be placed. Must be within the grid's row range.
The number of columns the image should span horizontally. Must be positive and not exceed the grid's column count.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageImageExtensions.DrawImageWithFixedWidth(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,PdfSharp.Drawing.XImage,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Draws the specified image onto the PDF grid page at the given column and row, scaling the image to match the page's resolution and maintaining its original width.
member.remarks: The image is scaled based on the page's resolution to ensure its width remains consistent with its original pixel width. The height is adjusted proportionally to preserve the image's aspect ratio.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the image will be drawn.
The image to draw on the grid page.
The column index in the grid where the left edge of the image will be placed.
The row index in the grid where the top edge of the image will be placed.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageImageExtensions.DrawImageWithFixedHeight(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Draws an image onto the PDF grid page, scaling it to fit a specified number of grid rows while maintaining its aspect ratio.
member.remarks: The image is scaled proportionally so its height matches the specified number of grid rows. The width is adjusted to preserve the original aspect ratio. The image is placed at the specified grid location. If the image file is invalid or cannot be loaded, an exception may be thrown.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the image will be drawn.
The file path of the image to be rendered. Must reference a valid image file.
The index of the leftmost grid column where the image will be positioned.
The index of the topmost grid row where the image will be positioned.
The number of grid rows the image's height should span. Must be positive.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageImageExtensions.DrawImage(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.String,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfHorizontalAlignment,PdfDocuments.PdfVerticalAlignment)
member.summary: Draws an image onto the specified PDF page within the given bounds, aligning it according to the provided horizontal and vertical alignment options.
member.param: The PDF page on which the image will be drawn.
The path to the image file to be rendered on the PDF page. Must be a valid file path to an image supported by the library.
The rectangular area, in PDF coordinates, where the image will be placed.
Specifies how the image is aligned horizontally within the bounds.
Specifies how the image is aligned vertically within the bounds.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageImageExtensions.DrawImage(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,PdfSharp.Drawing.XImage,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfHorizontalAlignment,PdfDocuments.PdfVerticalAlignment)
member.summary: Draws the specified image onto the PDF grid page, aligning it within the given bounds according to the specified horizontal and vertical alignment.
member.remarks: The image is scaled to fit the grid cell dimensions and positioned based on the alignment parameters. Use this method to place images precisely within a grid layout on a PDF page.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the image will be drawn.
The image to render onto the grid page.
The bounds within the grid page that define the area for image placement and alignment.
Specifies how the image is aligned horizontally within the provided bounds.
Specifies how the image is aligned vertically within the provided bounds.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageLineExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for drawing rectangles, lines, and grids on a PDF grid page using specified bounds, colors, and pens.
member.remarks: These extension methods simplify rendering shapes and lines on a PDF grid page by abstracting coordinate calculations and drawing logic. Methods support both filled and outlined rectangles, horizontal and vertical lines, and full grid rendering. All drawing operations are performed relative to the grid's cell structure, allowing for precise placement within the page layout. Thread safety is not guaranteed; ensure that drawing operations are performed in a single-threaded context.

member.param: The PDF grid page on which the filled rectangle will be drawn.

The color used to fill the rectangle.

member.summary: Draws a filled rectangle on the specified PDF grid page using the given grid coordinates and color.
member.remarks: The rectangle is drawn based on the grid's column and row indices, allowing alignment with grid cells. The rectangle will cover all cells from the specified left and top indices to the right and bottom indices, inclusive.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the rectangle will be drawn.
The index of the leftmost column of the rectangle within the grid.
The index of the topmost row of the rectangle within the grid.
The index of the rightmost column of the rectangle within the grid.
The index of the bottommost row of the rectangle within the grid.
The color used to fill the rectangle.

member.summary: Draws a rectangle on the specified PDF grid page using the given bounds, line weight, and color.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the rectangle will be drawn.
The bounds defining the position and size of the rectangle within the grid page.
The thickness of the rectangle's outline, in points. Must be greater than zero.
The color used for the rectangle's outline.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageLineExtensions.DrawRectangle(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,PdfSharp.Drawing.XPen)

member.summary: Draws a vertical line on the specified grid page between two rows at the given column edge.
member.param: The grid page on which the vertical line will be drawn.
The column index where the vertical line will be placed.

The index of the row where the vertical line ends.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageLineExtensions.DrawHorizontalLine(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,PdfDocuments.RowEdge,System.Double,PdfSharp.Drawing.XColor)
member.summary: Draws a horizontal line across the specified row of the grid on the PDF page.
member.remarks: The line is drawn from the left edge of the start column to the right edge of the end column. Use this method to visually separate rows or highlight specific sections within the grid.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the horizontal line will be drawn.

The index of the starting column for the line segment.
The index of the ending column for the line segment.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageLineExtensions.DrawGrid(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,PdfSharp.Drawing.XColor,System.Double)
member.summary: Draws a complete grid on the specified PDF page using the given color and line weight.
member.remarks: This method draws both horizontal and vertical lines to outline all rows and columns of the grid. Use this method to visually separate grid cells on the page. The operation does not modify the grid's data or structure.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the grid lines will be drawn. Must not be null.
The color used for the grid lines.
The thickness of the grid lines, in points. Must be greater than zero.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageLineExtensions.GetRect(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Calculates the rectangle that corresponds to the specified grid bounds on the given PDF page.
member.remarks: The returned rectangle reflects the position and size of the specified grid region within the PDF page. If the bounds specify columns or rows with zero or negative width or height, the rectangle will default to a size of 1 in the corresponding dimension.
member.param: The PDF grid page from which to determine the rectangle. Must not be null.
The grid bounds specifying the columns and rows for which to calculate the rectangle. Must not be null.
member.returns: An XRect representing the area covered by the specified bounds on the grid. The rectangle will have a minimum width and height of 1 if the calculated size is zero or negative.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageLineExtensions.GetRect(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates the rectangle that spans the specified grid columns and rows on the given PDF grid page.
member.remarks: Use this method to obtain the exact rectangle for a cell or range of cells in a PDF grid, which can be useful for drawing or layout operations. The rectangle coordinates are based on the grid's current layout.
member.param: The PDF grid page from which to obtain the grid coordinates.
The index of the leftmost column of the rectangle. Must be within the valid column range of the grid.
The index of the topmost row of the rectangle. Must be within the valid row range of the grid.
The index of the rightmost column of the rectangle. Must be greater than or equal to leftColumn and within the valid column range.
The index of the bottommost row of the rectangle. Must be greater than or equal to topRow and within the valid row range.
member.returns: An XRect representing the area defined by the specified columns and rows. The rectangle will have a minimum width and height of 1 if the calculated size is less than or equal to zero.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageTextExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for measuring and drawing text within a grid-based PDF page, including support for font selection, text formatting, color, and debug overlays.
member.remarks: These extension methods simplify text rendering and measurement on PDF pages that use a grid layout. They offer overloads for specifying fonts, bounds, and formatting options, and include features for debugging such as font detail overlays and outlines. Methods are designed to work with the grid structure, allowing precise placement and sizing of text elements.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageTextExtensions.MeasureText(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.String,System.Double,PdfSharp.Drawing.XFontStyleEx,System.String)
member.summary: Measures the size of the specified text when rendered on the given PDF grid page using the provided font family, size, and style.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the text measurement is performed.
The name of the font family to use for measuring the text.
The font size, in points, used for the measurement. Must be a positive value.
The font style to apply, such as bold or italic, when measuring the text.
The text to measure. If not specified, the default value is "Test".
member.returns: A PdfSize structure representing the width and height of the measured text in PDF units.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageTextExtensions.MeasureText(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,PdfSharp.Drawing.XFont,System.String)
member.summary: Calculates the number of grid rows and columns required to display the specified text using the given font on the provided PDF grid page.
member.remarks: The calculation is based on the current row height and column width of the grid. This method is useful for determining layout requirements before rendering text.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the text will be measured. Must not be null.
The font used to render the text. Determines the size and style of the measured text. Must not be null.
The text to measure. If null, an empty string is used. Defaults to "Test".
member.returns: A PdfSize object containing the number of rows and columns needed to fit the text within the grid, based on the font and page settings.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageTextExtensions.DrawText(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.String,PdfSharp.Drawing.XFont,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfSharp.Drawing.XStringFormat,PdfSharp.Drawing.XColor,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Draws the specified text within the given bounds on the PDF page using the provided font, formatting, and color. Optionally disables debug overlays such as font details and outline.
member.remarks: When debug overlays are enabled and not suppressed, the method may draw additional visual elements such as the font name and an outline around the text area. These overlays are useful for debugging layout and font selection.
member.param: The PDF page on which the text will be drawn.
The text to render. If null, an empty string is drawn.
The font used to render the text.
The area within which the text will be drawn, specified as PDF bounds.
The formatting options that control text alignment and layout.
The color used to render the text.
If set to , disables debug overlays such as font details and outline regardless of the page's debug mode.
member.returns: A representing the measured size of the rendered text.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfGridPageTextExtensions.DrawText(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,System.String,System.String,System.Double,PdfSharp.Drawing.XFontStyleEx,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfSharp.Drawing.XStringFormat,PdfSharp.Drawing.XColor)

member.param: The PDF page on which the text will be drawn.
The text string to render on the page.

The font size, in points, used to render the text. Must be greater than zero.
The font style to apply, such as bold or italic.
The rectangular area, in PDF coordinates, within which the text will be drawn.
The string formatting options that determine text alignment and layout within the bounds.
The color used to render the text.
member.returns: A PdfSize structure representing the size of the rendered text within the specified bounds.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfPageExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the PdfPage type to enhance functionality and convenience.
member.remarks: This class contains static methods that extend the PdfPage type, allowing for additional operations such as retrieving page resolution. All methods are intended to be used as extension methods and require a valid PdfPage instance.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfPageExtensions.GetPageResolution(PdfSharp.Pdf.PdfPage)
member.summary: Calculates the resolution of the specified PDF page in user units per point.
member.remarks: The resolution is calculated based on the ratio of the page's user unit value to its point value for both width and height. This can be useful for rendering or scaling operations where precise page measurements are required.
member.param: The PDF page for which to determine the resolution. Cannot be null.
member.returns: An XSize structure representing the width and height resolution of the page, measured in user units per point.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfSectionExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the IPdfSection<TModel> interface to support margin application and style resolution in PDF generation scenarios.
member.remarks: These extension methods simplify common operations when working with PDF sections, such as adjusting bounds for margins and resolving styles based on section configuration. Intended for use with implementations of IPdfSection<TModel> in PDF rendering workflows.

The PDF grid page context used for calculating the bounds.
The model instance associated with the section.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfSectionExtensions.ResolveStyle``1(PdfDocuments.IPdfSection{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Resolves and returns the style associated with the specified index for the given PDF section.
member.remarks: If the section contains no style names, the default style is returned. If the index exceeds the available style names, the first style is used.

member.param: The PDF section for which to resolve the style. Cannot be null.
The zero-based index of the style to resolve. If the index is out of range, the first style is used.
member.returns: A instance corresponding to the specified style index, or the default style if no styles are defined.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.ServiceCollectionExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for registering PDF-related services with an IServiceCollection.
member.remarks: These extension methods simplify the configuration of PDF generation and styling services in dependency injection containers. They are intended to be used during application startup to add required PDF services to the service collection.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddPdfDocuments(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Adds PDF document generation services to the specified service collection.
member.remarks: Registers the service with a scoped lifetime. Call this method during application startup to enable PDF generation features.
member.param: The service collection to which the PDF document generation services will be added. Cannot be null.
member.returns: The same instance of that was provided, to support method chaining.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddPdfStyleManager``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Adds a scoped PDF style manager service for the specified model type to the service collection.
member.remarks: Registers IPdfStyleManager<TModel>; with its default implementation PdfStyleManager<TModel>; using scoped lifetime. Call this method during application startup to enable PDF style management for the specified model type.
member.typeparam: The type of PDF model for which the style manager is registered. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.
member.param: The service collection to which the PDF style manager will be added.
member.returns: The same IServiceCollection instance so that additional calls can be chained.

member.typeparam: The type of the PDF model for which the style manager is registered. Must implement .
member.param: The service collection to which the PDF style manager will be added.
The instance of to register for dependency injection.
member.returns: The same instance so that additional calls can be chained.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.Style
member.summary: Provides static methods for creating and configuring PDF style builders for document elements. Enables fluent customization of visual properties such as font, color, spacing, alignment, and borders for models implementing the IPdfModel interface.
member.remarks: Use the methods in this class to construct and modify style definitions for PDF elements in a type-safe and composable manner. Styles can be built incrementally by chaining configuration methods, allowing for flexible reuse and adaptation. All methods are thread-safe when used with separate style builder instances. The class supports both property binding and direct value assignment for style attributes.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Style.Create``1
member.summary: Creates a new style builder instance for the specified PDF model type.
member.typeparam: The type of PDF model for which the style builder is created. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.
member.returns: A style builder instance for the specified PDF model type.

member.param: The style to copy. Cannot be null.
member.returns: A new instance with properties copied from the specified style.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for working with XColor and related color types, including conversions, color manipulation, and utility functions.
member.remarks: This static class offers methods to convert between XColor and System.Drawing.Color, generate random colors, calculate color contrast, and perform color space transformations. The methods are intended to simplify common color operations when working with XColor and related types.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions.Contrast(PdfSharp.Drawing.XColor,PdfSharp.Drawing.XColor)
member.summary: Calculates the contrast value between two colors based on their brightness.
member.remarks: The contrast is determined by the difference in brightness between the two colors, scaled to a 0–255 range. This method can be used to assess the visual distinction between foreground and background colors.
member.param: The first color to compare. Represents one of the two colors for which the contrast is calculated.
The second color to compare. Represents the other color for which the contrast is calculated.
member.returns: A double value representing the absolute difference in brightness between the two colors. Higher values indicate greater contrast.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions.RandomColor
member.summary: Generates a random color with full opacity.
member.remarks: Each call produces a different color. The alpha channel is always set to fully opaque.
member.returns: A new XColor instance with random red, green, and blue channel values and an alpha value of 255.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions.ToGdiColor(PdfSharp.Drawing.XColor)
member.summary: Converts an XColor structure to a System.Drawing.Color structure.
member.remarks: The alpha component of the XColor is scaled from a 0.0–1.0 range to a 0–255 range in the resulting System.Drawing.Color.
member.param: The XColor value to convert to a System.Drawing.Color.
member.returns: A System.Drawing.Color structure that represents the equivalent color of the specified XColor.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions.ToXColor(System.Drawing.Color)
member.summary: Converts a System.Drawing.Color instance to an XColor instance with the same ARGB values.
member.param: The System.Drawing.Color to convert to an XColor.
member.returns: An XColor instance that represents the same color as the specified System.Drawing.Color.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions.ToHsl(System.Drawing.Color)
member.summary: Converts the specified RGB color to its equivalent HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) representation.
member.remarks: The returned HSL values are calculated according to the standard RGB-to-HSL conversion algorithm. Hue is expressed in degrees (0–360), while saturation and lightness are in the range 0–1. This method is useful for color manipulation scenarios where HSL representation is preferred over RGB.
member.param: The color to convert. The RGB components are used to calculate the HSL values.
member.returns: An Hsl structure representing the hue, saturation, and lightness values corresponding to the input color.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions.IsNearTo(System.Double,System.Double,System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified double-precision floating-point value is near to the current value within a given number of decimal digits.
member.remarks: This method is useful for comparing floating-point values where exact equality is not required due to potential rounding errors. It is commonly used in scenarios where small differences between values are acceptable.
member.param: The current double-precision floating-point value to compare.
The double-precision floating-point value to compare against.

The number of decimal digits to use for the comparison. Must be non-negative. The comparison considers the values near if their difference is less than 1 divided by 10 to the power of this value. The default is 3.
member.returns: true if the two values are within the specified number of decimal digits of each other; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions.WithLuminosity(PdfSharp.Drawing.XColor,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns a new color based on the specified color with its luminosity component set to the specified value.
member.remarks: This method does not modify the original color. The resulting color may be clipped if the luminosity value is outside the valid range.
member.param: The source color to modify.
The luminosity value to set, typically in the range 0.0 (black) to 1.0 (white).
member.returns: A new XColor instance with the same hue and saturation as the original color, but with the specified luminosity.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.XColorExtensions.ToXColor(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a hexadecimal color string to an equivalent XColor instance.
member.remarks: If the input string contains six hexadecimal digits, the color is interpreted as opaque (alpha value of 255). If eight digits are provided, the first two represent the alpha channel.
member.param: A string representing the color in hexadecimal format. The string may optionally start with a '#' and must be in either RRGGBB or AARRGGBB format.
member.returns: An XColor instance corresponding to the specified hexadecimal color value.
member.exception: Thrown if hexColor is null, empty, or consists only of white-space characters.
Thrown if hexColor is not in a valid RRGGBB or AARRGGBB hexadecimal format.

member.summary: Gets the relative widths for each column in the layout as a bindable property.
member.remarks: The array represents proportional widths, where each value determines the relative size of a column compared to others. The sum of all values does not need to equal 1; proportions are calculated based on the array contents. This property is typically used to control column sizing in dynamic layouts.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.IPdfStyleManager`1

member.remarks: Implementations of this interface allow for organized storage and retrieval of PDF styles by name. The interface extends IDictionary, enabling standard collection operations alongside specialized style management methods.

An additional state object that can be used to influence the processing of the value.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.BindProperty`2
member.summary: Represents a bindable property for a PDF model, allowing dynamic resolution and optional transformation of property values within a PDF grid context.
member.remarks: BindProperty enables flexible property binding and transformation for PDF generation scenarios. The property value can be resolved based on the current grid page and model, and optionally modified using a hook action. This class supports both synchronous and asynchronous resolution, as well as implicit conversions for ease of use. Thread safety is not guaranteed; instances should not be shared across threads without external synchronization.
member.typeparam: The type of the property value to be resolved and bound.
The type of the PDF model implementing the IPdfModel interface.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the BindProperty class.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the BindProperty class with the specified binding action.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.BindProperty`2.OnResolve(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`1,System.Object)
member.summary: Resolves and returns a property value based on the specified grid page and model, optionally applying a hook for additional processing.

The model instance from which the property value is resolved.
An optional state object passed to the hook for additional processing. May be null.
member.returns: The resolved property value of type TProperty. If no action is defined, returns the default value for TProperty.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.BindProperty`2.op_Implicit(`0)~PdfDocuments.BindProperty{`0,`1}
member.summary: Creates a new instance of BindProperty<TProperty, TModel> from the specified property value.
member.remarks: This implicit conversion allows a property value to be used directly as a BindProperty<TProperty, TModel> without explicit construction. The resulting BindProperty always returns the provided value, regardless of the model or property context.
member.param: The property value to bind. Cannot be null if TProperty is a non-nullable type.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.BindProperty`2.op_Implicit(PdfDocuments.BindPropertyAction{`0,`1})~PdfDocuments.BindProperty{`0,`1}
member.summary: Converts a specified action delegate to a BindProperty instance, enabling property binding with the provided action.
member.remarks: Use this operator to simplify the creation of BindProperty instances from action delegates. This conversion allows for more concise property binding syntax.
member.param: The action delegate that defines how the property should be bound to the model. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.BindProperty`2.op_Implicit(PdfDocuments.BindProperty{`0,`1})~PdfDocuments.BindPropertyAction{`0,`1}
member.summary: Defines an implicit conversion from a BindProperty instance to a BindPropertyAction instance, enabling assignment without explicit casting.
member.remarks: This operator allows seamless usage of BindProperty objects in contexts where a BindPropertyAction is expected. If the source instance is null, the resulting BindPropertyAction will have a null Action property.
member.param: The BindProperty instance to convert. Cannot be null.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.GlobalPdfDocumentsSettings

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.Hsl
member.summary: Represents a color in the HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) color space.
member.remarks: The HSL color model is commonly used for color manipulation and selection, as it separates color information (hue) from intensity (lightness) and purity (saturation). The values for hue, saturation, and lightness are not validated; callers should ensure they are within the expected ranges for correct color conversion.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Hsl.#ctor(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Hsl class with the specified hue, saturation, and lightness values.
member.param: The hue component of the color, typically in degrees from 0 to 360.
The saturation component of the color, as a value between 0 and 1.
The lightness component of the color, as a value between 0 and 1.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.Hsl.H
member.summary: Gets or sets the value of H.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.Hsl.S
member.summary: Gets or sets the value of S.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.Hsl.L
member.summary: Gets or sets the value of L.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Hsl.ToColor
member.summary: Converts the current HSL color representation to a corresponding RGB color.
member.remarks: This method performs a conversion from HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) to RGB (Red, Green, Blue) color space. The resulting color is suitable for use in graphics APIs that require RGB values. The conversion preserves the perceived color as closely as possible.
member.returns: A Color instance representing the equivalent RGB color. The returned color will have its alpha channel set to 255.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.Hsl.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string representation of the HSL color using formatted numeric values.
member.remarks: The returned string is formatted as "H, S%, L%", where H is the hue, S is the saturation, and L is the lightness. This format is useful for displaying HSL color values in a human-readable form.
member.returns: A string containing the hue as a number with one decimal place, and the saturation and lightness as percentages.

The bounds are expressed as integer values for rows and columns, which can be useful for mapping content or extracting regions from structured PDF layouts.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfGrid
member.summary: Represents a rectangular grid layout for arranging elements within a PDF document. Provides methods and properties for calculating cell positions and dimensions based on the specified grid size and offsets.
member.remarks: Use the grid to determine the coordinates and sizes of individual cells or groups of cells when rendering content in a PDF. The grid supports configurable width, height, row and column counts, and optional offsets for positioning. All calculations assume uniform cell sizes across the grid. This class is useful for scenarios such as table layouts, form fields, or structured content placement.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1
member.summary: Provides a configurable set of style properties for PDF elements, supporting data binding to a model type.
member.remarks: Use this class to define visual appearance and layout for PDF components, such as fonts, colors, spacing, and alignment. Each property supports binding to values from the specified model type, enabling dynamic styling based on model data.
member.typeparam: The type of model to which style properties are bound. Must implement .

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.RelativeHeight
member.summary: Gets or sets the relative height value for the model, expressed as a proportion of the total available height.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.RelativeWidths
member.summary: Gets or sets the relative widths for each column in the layout.
member.remarks: Each value in the array represents the proportional width of a column. The sum of all values determines the overall distribution. Values must be non-negative. Adjusting this property changes how space is allocated among columns.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.Font
member.summary: Gets or sets the font used for rendering text in the bound model.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.Margin
member.summary: Gets or sets the margin spacing applied to the PDF content.
member.remarks: Use this property to specify the amount of space between the content and the edges of the PDF page. Adjusting the margin can affect layout and readability.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.Padding
member.summary: Gets or sets the padding applied to the content, specifying the space between the content and its boundaries.
member.remarks: The padding is defined using the four sides: left, top, right, and bottom. Adjusting the padding affects the layout and spacing of the content within its container.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.ForegroundColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the foreground color used for rendering the associated model.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.BackgroundColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the background color for the element.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.BorderWidth
member.summary: Gets or sets the width of the border, in pixels, for the associated model element.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.BorderColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the border color for the element.
member.remarks: The border color determines the visual appearance of the element's border. Setting this property to a transparent color will render the border invisible. The value can be data-bound to dynamically update the border color based on the model.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.TextAlignment
member.summary: Gets or sets the text alignment format applied to the content.
member.remarks: Use this property to control how text is aligned within the element. The default value aligns text to the left and centers it vertically.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.ParagraphAlignment
member.summary: Gets or sets the paragraph alignment for the bound model.
member.remarks: Use this property to specify how text within a paragraph is aligned, such as left, right, center, or justified. The default alignment is justified. Changing the alignment affects the visual layout of paragraph content.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfStyle`1.CellPadding
member.summary: Gets or sets the padding applied to each cell within the table.
member.remarks: Cell padding determines the space between the cell content and its borders. Adjusting this property affects the visual layout and spacing of table cells.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfStyleManager`1
member.summary: Manages a collection of named PDF styles for a specific model type, providing access and customization of font and formatting options used in PDF generation.
member.remarks: Use PdfStyleManager to define, retrieve, and replace styles by name for consistent PDF formatting. The manager includes predefined styles such as "Default" and "Debug" to facilitate common scenarios. Styles can be customized for different model types, enabling flexible document appearance. Thread safety is not guaranteed; synchronize access if used concurrently.
member.typeparam: The model type for which PDF styles are managed. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.

member.name: F:PdfDocuments.PdfStyleManager`1.Default
member.summary: Represents the default string value used for configuration or identification purposes.

member.name: F:PdfDocuments.PdfStyleManager`1.Debug
member.summary: Represents the string value used to indicate debug-level logging.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfStyleManager`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PdfStyleManager class using the default font name specified in global PDF document settings.
member.remarks: This constructor provides a convenient way to create a PdfStyleManager with standard font settings. Use this overload when custom font configuration is not required.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfStyleManager`1.#ctor(System.String,System.Double)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PdfStyleManager class with the specified default font name.
member.remarks: The constructor adds a default style and a debug style using the provided font name. Use this to ensure consistent font settings across generated PDF styles.
member.param: The name of the font to use as the default for styles managed by this instance. Cannot be null or empty.
The size of the font in points. Default is 8. Use this to specify the default font size for styles.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfStyleManager`1.GetStyle(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the style associated with the specified name, or returns the default style if the name is not found.
member.param: The name of the style to retrieve. If the specified name does not exist, the default style is returned.
member.returns: The style corresponding to the specified name, or the default style if the name is not present.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfStyleManager`1.Replace(System.String,PdfDocuments.PdfStyle{`0})
member.summary: Replaces the style associated with the specified name. If a style with the given name already exists, it is removed and replaced; otherwise, the style is added.
member.param: The key name identifying the style to replace or add. Cannot be null.
The style to associate with the specified name. Cannot be null.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfVerticalAlignment
member.summary: Specifies the vertical alignment options for content within a PDF element.
member.remarks: Use this enumeration to control how content is positioned vertically, such as aligning text or images to the top, center, or bottom of a container. The alignment affects layout and appearance when rendering PDF documents.

member.name: F:PdfDocuments.PdfVerticalAlignment.Top
member.summary: Represents the top-level element or object in the hierarchy.

member.name: F:PdfDocuments.PdfVerticalAlignment.Center
member.summary: Gets or sets the center point of the object.

member.name: F:PdfDocuments.PdfVerticalAlignment.Bottom
member.summary: Represents the bottom position or value in the enumeration.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.RowEdge
member.summary: Specifies the vertical edge of a row, such as the top or bottom position, in a layout or hierarchical structure.

member.name: F:PdfDocuments.RowEdge.Top
member.summary: Represents the top-level element or object in the hierarchy.

member.name: F:PdfDocuments.RowEdge.Bottom
member.summary: Represents the bottom alignment or position in a layout or enumeration.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.SignatureOptions`1
member.summary: Represents configurable options for customizing signature fields in a PDF model.
member.remarks: Use this class to specify text, image, and date-related properties for signature fields when generating or editing PDF documents. Each property is bound to the specified PDF model type, allowing dynamic customization based on model data.
member.typeparam: The type of PDF model to which the signature options are bound. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.SignatureOptions`1.SignatureText
member.summary: Gets or sets the signature text associated with the model.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.SignatureOptions`1.SignatureImage
member.summary: Gets or sets the image data representing the signature for the model.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.SignatureOptions`1.DateLabel
member.summary: Gets or sets the label text used to display the date field in the user interface.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.SignatureOptions`1.Date
member.summary: Gets or sets the date value to be bound to the model.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.SignatureOptions`1.Offset
member.summary: Gets or sets the offset of the signature image within the PDF document.
member.remarks: The offset is specified as a size in columns and rows, allowing precise placement of the signature image relative to the document layout. Adjust this property to control the horizontal and vertical positioning of the signature image.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfContentSection`1
member.summary: Represents a section of PDF content that is rendered using a model of type .
member.typeparam: The type of model used to provide data for rendering the PDF content section. Must implement .

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridSection`2
member.summary: Represents a section in a PDF document that displays a data grid with configurable columns and items, supporting dynamic binding and formatting.
member.remarks: Use this class to define tabular data layouts within a PDF section. Columns and items can be bound to model properties, and column formatting, styles, and widths are customizable. Supports both static and dynamic binding scenarios for headers, cell values, and styles.
member.typeparam: The type of the PDF model used for binding and rendering section content.
The type of the items displayed in the data grid rows.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridSection`2.DataColumns
member.summary: Gets the collection of data columns used to define the structure and content of the grid.
member.remarks: Each column in the collection represents a field or property from the model type and controls how data is displayed in the grid. The collection is read-only; to modify the columns, add or remove items from the existing list.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridSection`2.Items
member.summary: Gets or sets the collection of items to be bound to the model.
member.remarks: Use this property to provide the set of items that will be displayed or processed in the context of the model. The property supports binding scenarios where the items are dynamically updated or retrieved from external sources.

member.summary: Adds a new data column to the grid with the specified header, binding expression, relative width, format, and style settings.
member.remarks: The column is added to the grid's collection of data columns. Use this method to configure column appearance and data binding in a flexible manner.
member.typeparam: The type of the property to bind to the column's data cells.
member.param: A bindable property representing the column header text. The value is displayed as the column's header.
An expression that identifies the property of the data item to bind to the column's cells.
A bindable property specifying the relative width of the column. The value determines the column's proportional size within the grid.
A bindable property specifying the string format to apply to the column's cell values. The format is used when rendering cell content.
A bindable property specifying the style name to apply to the column header. The style controls the appearance of the header cell.
A bindable property specifying the style name to apply to the column's data cells. The style controls the appearance of the cell content.
member.returns: A PdfDataGridColumn<TModel>; instance representing the newly added data column.

member.summary: Adds a new data column to the grid with the specified header, binding expression, relative width, format, and style settings.
member.remarks: Use this method to dynamically configure columns in a data grid, including header text, binding, formatting, and styling. The delegates allow customization based on the model, enabling flexible column definitions.
member.typeparam: The type of the property to bind to the column's data cells.
member.param: A delegate that provides the column header text based on the model.
An expression that identifies the property of the item to bind as the column's data source.
A delegate that determines the relative width of the column based on the model.
A delegate that specifies the string format for displaying cell values in the column.
A delegate that provides the style name to apply to the column header based on the model.
A delegate that provides the style name to apply to the column's data cells based on the model.
member.returns: A PdfDataGridColumn<TModel>; instance representing the newly added data column.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridSection`2.OnRenderAsync(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Renders the grid page content asynchronously using the specified model and layout bounds.
member.remarks: This method renders both header and data rows based on the provided model and column definitions. The rendering respects the specified bounds and column widths. Override this method to customize rendering behavior for derived grid types.
member.param: The PDF grid page to render content onto.
The model instance providing data for rendering.
The layout bounds defining the area and columns available for rendering.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous rendering operation. The task result is if rendering was successful; otherwise, .

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridSection`2.FormattedValue(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridColumn{`0},`1)
member.summary: Returns the formatted string representation of the specified property value for a grid cell.
member.remarks: If a string format is defined for the column, it is applied to the property value. Otherwise, the value is converted to a string using default formatting.
member.param: The grid page context used for formatting operations.
The model instance associated with the current grid row.
The column definition containing formatting and member information.
The data item from which the property value is retrieved.
member.returns: A string containing the formatted value of the property for the specified grid cell.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridSection`2.OnRenderHeaderColumn(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfStyle{`0},PdfDocuments.PdfTextElement{`0})
member.summary: Renders a header column in the PDF grid using the specified model, bounds, style, and text element.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the header column will be rendered.
The data model instance used to provide content for the header column.
The bounds within which the header column will be rendered.
The style to apply when rendering the header column.
The text element responsible for rendering the header column content.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridSection`2.OnRenderDataColumn(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds,PdfDocuments.PdfStyle{`0},PdfDocuments.PdfTextElement{`0},`1)
member.summary: Renders a data column within the grid using the specified text element, style, and bounds.
member.param: The grid page on which the data column will be rendered.
The model instance providing data context for rendering.
The bounds that define the area where the data column will be rendered.
The style to apply when rendering the data column.
The text element responsible for rendering the content of the data column.
The item representing the data to be rendered in the column.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfDataGridSection`2.OnCalculateHeightAsync(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Asynchronously calculates the total height, in rows, required to render the grid page with its headers and data rows.
member.remarks: The calculated height accounts for the tallest header and data row in each column, multiplied by the number of data items. The result may be used to determine page layout or pagination when rendering the grid.
member.param: The PDF grid page context used for layout calculations.
The data model instance providing values for the grid.
The bounds within which the grid content should be measured.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains the total height, in rows, needed to render the grid including headers and data rows.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfEmptySection`1
member.summary: Represents a PDF section that contains no content. This class can be used to insert an empty section into a PDF document template.
member.typeparam: The type of the model associated with the PDF section. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfHeaderContentSection`1
member.summary: Represents a template section for rendering header content within a PDF document, using a specified model type.
member.remarks: This class is intended for use in PDF generation scenarios where a header section needs to be rendered with customizable content and layout. It manages the layout and rendering of header text and background, and positions child sections directly below the header. The header appearance is determined by resolved styles and model data. Thread safety is not guaranteed; instances should not be shared across threads without external synchronization.
member.typeparam: The type of model used to provide data for the header content. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfHeaderContentSection`1.OnLayoutChildrenAsync(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Arranges the child elements of the grid section asynchronously, positioning them relative to the header within the specified bounds.
member.remarks: This method positions child elements directly below the header section, adjusting their bounds based on the header's size. Only the first child is affected if multiple children exist.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the layout operation is performed.
The model containing data used for layout calculations.
The bounds within which the child elements should be arranged.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous layout operation. The result is if the layout was applied; otherwise, .

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfHeaderContentSection`1.OnShouldDrawBackground
member.summary: Determines whether the background should be drawn for the current control.
member.remarks: Override this method to customize background rendering behavior for derived controls.
member.returns: Always returns , indicating that the background will not be drawn.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfHeaderContentSection`1.OnRenderAsync(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Renders the header content asynchronously onto the specified PDF grid page using the provided model and bounds.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the header will be rendered.
The model containing data used for rendering the header content.
The bounds within which the header should be rendered on the page.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is if the header was rendered successfully.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfHeaderContentSection`1.GetHeaderSize(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0)
member.summary: Calculates the size required to render the header text, including padding, for the specified page and model.
member.remarks: Override this method to customize header sizing logic for different grid layouts or model types.
member.param: The page context used to measure the header text and resolve styles.
The model instance used to resolve the header text and style information.
member.returns: A PdfSize structure representing the total size needed to display the header, including text and padding.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfHeaderContentSection`1.GetHeaderRect(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Calculates the bounding rectangle for the header area of a grid page based on the specified model and layout bounds.
member.param: The grid page for which the header rectangle is being calculated.
The data model used to determine header layout and content.
The layout bounds representing the area available for the grid on the page.
member.returns: A PdfBounds object representing the position and size of the header area within the specified bounds.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfHorizontalStackSection`1
member.summary: Represents a section in a PDF document that arranges its child sections horizontally, distributing available space according to relative widths or evenly when unspecified.
member.remarks: Child sections are laid out side by side within the section's bounds. Sections with specified relative widths receive proportional space, while others share remaining space equally. Padding and layout are applied to each child section. This class is typically used to compose complex PDF layouts by stacking multiple sections horizontally.
member.typeparam: The model type used for rendering and layout operations. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfHorizontalStackSection`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PdfHorizontalStackSection class, which represents a section that arranges PDF elements horizontally.
member.remarks: Use this constructor to create a horizontal stack section for organizing PDF content side by side. This section can be added to a PDF document to group elements in a horizontal layout.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfHorizontalStackSection`1.#ctor(PdfDocuments.IPdfSection{`0}[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PdfHorizontalStackSection class with the specified child sections.
member.remarks: Each child section will be rendered side by side in the order provided. Use this constructor to compose complex layouts from multiple sections.
member.param: An array of child sections to be arranged horizontally within this section. Cannot be null or contain null elements.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfHorizontalStackSection`1.OnLayoutChildrenAsync(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Arranges and lays out child sections within the specified grid page using the provided model and bounds.
member.remarks: Child sections are arranged left to right, with column widths determined by relative or absolute specifications. Padding is applied to each section before layout. If any section fails to layout, the operation returns .
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the child sections are to be laid out.
The model instance containing data used for layout calculations and rendering.
The bounds defining the area and column/row constraints for layout within the grid page.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is if all child sections were successfully laid out; otherwise, .

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfImageSection`1
member.summary: Represents a PDF section that renders an image, using a data-bound path from the model.
member.remarks: This section draws an image left-aligned and vertically centered within the specified bounds, using the resolved image path from the model. The image is only rendered if the resolved path is not null, empty, or whitespace, and the file exists at the specified location.
member.typeparam: The type of the model used to provide data for the section. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfImageSection`1.Image
member.summary: Gets or sets the image source associated with the model.
member.remarks: The image source is typically a URL or file path that identifies the image to display for the model. The exact usage depends on the binding context and how the property is consumed in the application.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfImageSection`1.OnRenderAsync(PdfDocuments.PdfGridPage,`0,PdfDocuments.PdfBounds)
member.summary: Renders an image onto the specified PDF grid page using the provided model and layout bounds.
member.remarks: The image is rendered left-aligned and vertically centered within the specified bounds, using padding and style information resolved from the model. If the resolved image path is null, empty, or the file does not exist, no image is rendered.
member.param: The PDF grid page on which the image will be rendered.
The data model used to resolve image path and styling information.
The layout bounds that define the area within which the image is rendered.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous render operation. The result is true if rendering was attempted.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfKeyValueItem`1
member.summary: Represents a key-value pair for PDF data binding, where the value can be a static string or a bindable property associated with a model.
member.remarks: Use this class to define key-value pairs for PDF generation scenarios, supporting both static and dynamic values. The value can be bound to a property or action on the model, enabling flexible data extraction for PDF fields.
member.typeparam: The type of model associated with the value binding. Must implement the IPdfModel interface.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfKeyValueItem`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PdfKeyValueItem class.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfKeyValueItem`1.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PdfKeyValueItem class with the specified key and value.
member.param: The key associated with the item. Cannot be null.
The value associated with the item. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfKeyValueItem`1.#ctor(System.String,PdfDocuments.BindProperty{System.String,`0})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PdfKeyValueItem class with the specified key and value binding.
member.param: The key associated with the value. Cannot be null.
A binding that provides the value associated with the key. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:PdfDocuments.PdfKeyValueItem`1.#ctor(System.String,PdfDocuments.BindPropertyAction{System.String,`0})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PdfKeyValueItem class with the specified key and value binding action.
member.param: The key associated with the item. Cannot be null.
The action that binds a string value to the model. Cannot be null.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfKeyValueItem`1.Key
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier associated with this instance.

member.name: P:PdfDocuments.PdfKeyValueItem`1.Value
member.summary: Gets or sets the bound value associated with the model.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfKeyValueSection`1
member.summary: Represents a PDF section that displays a collection of key-value pairs using a specified model type.
member.remarks: Use this class to render structured key-value data within a PDF document. Each item in the section is displayed with its key and corresponding value, formatted according to the resolved styles. The section is suitable for scenarios where labeled values need to be presented, such as metadata, summaries, or property lists.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfOverlayStackSection`1

member.remarks: Use this class to group and overlay multiple PDF sections so that each child section is rendered on top of the others within the specified bounds. Only child sections whose ShouldRender condition evaluates to true will be included in the overlay. This is useful for scenarios where layered content or composite visual elements are required in a PDF document.

member.name: T:PdfDocuments.PdfSectionTemplate`1
member.summary: Represents an abstract template for a PDF section that supports hierarchical composition, styling, and data binding for rendering PDF documents.
member.remarks: PdfSectionTemplate provides a base for creating structured PDF sections with customizable layout, rendering, and style management. Sections can contain child sections, support conditional rendering, and bind properties to model data. Use this class as a foundation for building reusable and extensible PDF document components.
